text: Problem: The difference between two acute angles of a right-angled triangle is pi over 5 radians. Find the angles in degrees.
Let the two acute angles be A and C. Their difference is pi over 5 radians, which is 36 degrees.
Since the triangle is right-angled, the sum of the two acute angles is exactly 90 degrees.
Solving this system of equations, adding them gives twice A equals 126, so A is 63 degrees. Subtracting gives C equals 27 degrees.
